pubmed_links: [Curiel et al. (2017) TUBB4A mutations result in specific neuronal and oligodendrocytic defects matching distinct phenotypes](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28973395/); [Tonduti et al. (2016) TUBB4A-related hypomyelinating leukodystrophy: new insights from 12 patients](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26643067/); [Gavazzi et al. (2025) Natural history of variable subtypes in pediatric-onset TUBB4A-related leukodystrophy](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39951964/)
summary: TUBB4A encodes beta-tubulin 4A, essential for microtubule formation during brain development; pathogenic variants cause H-ABC leukodystrophy characterized by intellectual disability, developmental delay, and motor deficits, with autism spectrum features reported in ~39% of affected individuals. TUBB4A is a well-established cause of severe pediatric neurodevelopmental disease with prominent cognitive and neurodevelopmental impairment. (well-established)
